item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Look up Bright's 001 proposal which this is inspired by. The foundation DOES commit genocide once, against this exact type of being. If you consider multiple 001 canons, then the Foundation committed it twice, the other in…..I think Gear's proposal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are the Fae.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it up to the last O5 note which switched it to a -1 from me because it gives the impression that the Foundation is the cause of a full scale genocide, which I don't see happening, at least not purposefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ignore, misread original statement. My bad

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Addendum C: Due to events in previous tests, the entities are believed to share classification with an existing SCP object. The following test was conducted using a D-Class, D-00131, equipped with leather bag filled with iron dust. A female tortoise, Testudo graeca, was introduced as the SCP-1826 host. The tortoise grew small horns and vanished before approaching the subject 20 minutes after it was introduced to the building.

The "existing SCP object" mentioned here is meant to be the Fae from Bright's Proposal. That's now an actual SCP (4000), which has one very memorable feature - you cannot refer to it by name. Just like how this doesn't refer to the other SCP object by name.

This line, in particular, has aged very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m kind of mad the image deleted. I really thought it added to the SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going farther down that rabbit hole, spanish moss got its scientific name because it looks like a Usnea Lichen. So its a plant that looks like a type of lichen that gets called tree moss. Hooray for colloquial names. X3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might be a little late for a nitpick like this, but Spanish moss isn't actually a species of moss; it's an epiphyte belonging to Bromeliaceae.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The object class is Neutralized, but that seems to be the only explicit reference to this thing having changed its general behavior. The Incident Logs show that something way outside the usual pattern happened in response to the iron test, sure, but that's all- there's no indication that the pattern hasn't continued. And since the Description and Special Containment Procedures are still in the present tense, I get the impression that the whole females-get-transformed-and-hunt-males-to-the-death thing is still going on every March as before.

If you meant to indicate that that pattern stopped after the iron test, I suggest adding some text that says so. You can find examples of similar endings in SCP-1378, SCP-1762, SCP-1777, SCP-2180, and SCP-2350.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A broken window on the third floor suggests that the death was caused by impact with the ground.

Are Foundation's body doctors that stupid? They see a splattered body, they conclude the damage was likely caused by lupus or food poisoning. Maybe change it to something along the lines of 'Death was caused by impact with the ground; judging by the broken window on the third floor, the victim fell from there'.

SCP-1826 pulls out an object resembling an AK-47
Further investigation traced the gun back to a resident of Belarus believed to have gone missing in the area in 2005.

AK-47 is quite the stereotype and a common name for a variety of guns, but it cannot be "of modern manufacture". I think it would be better of you change it to AK-74, the model used by contemporary Belorussian military. Maybe note that the person missing was a conscript or otherwise involved with the army, as civilians are not allowed to own automatic weapons in Belarus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, the one in the picture is Sanders or D-00130?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes I know there are explanations but I did I have the weirdest feeling that someone just happened to find a really awesome picture and then created the most weird as fuck SCP just to go along with it. And, oddly enough, it's captivating enough that I read through the end and upvoted. ;P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same; it's called "hunter.jpg" in the source code, but the actual .jpg is called 8269 or something. So, yeah…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Picture isn't working for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…and during any other time during the year.

One of those "during"s should go, dealer's choice.
-The picture is still borked.
-They have confirmed that this is not a new anomaly, but rather an additional instance of a previously classified thing. So, bureaucratically speaking, shouldn't the 1826 designation be rescinded and this document added to the scp-blackbox file? I have no idea how to execute that without completly warping the flow, but the unrealistic filing did jangle, just a bit.

I really like these fae. They are adapting and tryign new things, not followign teh old ways by rote, which is teh traditional depiction. Intelligent faeries is a refreshing twist-up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pic is missing, otherwise would upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I upvoted at "The fire ant grew very small horns"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly weird, helped only somewhat by comments in this thread. The reasons why it's Neutralized aren't addressed where they should be, i.e., the description is still in present tense and the containment procedures are in place. It sounds like it's still active until you get to the bottom… I think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed the flow of this… the reasoning for the elimination of the SCP has changed, and I wrote the fae to appear less modern. The O5 comment is more of a sidenote now. The Foundation tests them with iron because they appear similar to an existing object. They try to breach containment because they feel like they're in danger, forcing the Foundation's hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I censored out the O5's mention and removed the enter passcode thing, it is heavily implied.

I'm still up in the air about the tactical procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clarified the wording here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tryed to fix some of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mack voting on SCPs? What is this sorcery?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not voting on this. There's potential, but it seems too unfocused right now. Needs some polishing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Feeling torn. On the one hand, fae-based SCPs are interesting. On the other hand, it's made way too explicit by the narrative; the O5 calling them by name just killed it for me. The tactical procedures are also slightly absurd to me, and collapsibles using the "enter passcode" gimmick are a real mood-killer for me. I don't even feel the collapsible is necessary in this case.

Tentative downvote; I'd like to see this improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As Heat said, I also was not clear on the specific thing that "SCP-1826" refers to. Other than that nitpick, I enjoyed it both as just a "Wild Hunt" SCP and as the real meaning once you read the extras.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1826 is a phenomenon taking place an office building in Oleksandriia, Ukraine

"Taking place an…" I think you may be missing a word, or it may be misspelled. Otherwise, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
similar in appearance to that of Spanish moss Tillandsia

This bothered me a bit. I think it would flow better if it were either "whose appearance is similar to that of Spanish moss etc." or "similar in appearance to Spanish moss etc."

Also, I was slightly confused as to what exactly "SCP-1826" referred to. The containment procedures seem to imply that it is the actual building, whereas the description itself uses it to designate either the phenomenon as a whole or the entity possessing those who enter the building during the month of March.

Edit: Upon rereading, this sentence also jumped up at me:

No anomalous properties were present in the gun, and is of modern manufacture with the serial number 196001430, which trace back to a resident of Belarus believed to have gone missing in the area in 2005.

"Is" has no grammatical subject (it's fairly obvious you're referring to the gun, but the construction is rather jarring), and "trace" should probably be "traces" since "the serial number" is singular.

Final edit, I swear: Can one really be armed with a gas mask? Wouldn't something like "equipped" be more appropriate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could do this&that SCPS were you could view two different versions… it doesn't look like I'm gonna have to rewrite this. That was actually my intention before I felt I needed a stronger hook.

Then again, if you only read it to so far, technically it is a played straight Wild Hunt SCP. *shrugs*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll drop this here too. It just feels like there's too much going on. It feels like a whole lot fo stuff tacked on, and expanded, where if you had just focused more on the core concept, it woulda been fantastic. I would have liked this more if it was played straighter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well, I think
– hide block

So, this is the beginning of a story, not the story in its entirety. I guess that does make more sense. And sort of fits with an idea for an -EX I had. Interestingly done.

Out of curiosity, where do the other Fae SCPs besides Bright's proposal (SCP-1323, SCP-562, possibly others) tie into this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Faeries εi3
– hide block

They're the remnants of the Fae (In my head canon) that were destroyed, and they're trying to make a resurgence. They purposely tried to attract the foundation's attention, obtaining SCP classification and made their way to a secure facility inside of the wolves.

They want control of the Factory, or simply want power again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it quite a bit. Certainly something different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed these points, but I left the gun producing vague.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sure, but...
– hide block

I figured the Wild Hunt thing was a fake (it reminded me of the fake Brit from SCP-1577), but I'm a little unclear exactly what they're going for. Trying to infiltrate the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers, simple explanation.
– hide block

The whole "Wild Hunt" thing was a facade. They're basically trying to mimic the germanic lore so the foundation has that expectation. They escaped on the wolves, leaving a few fae behind to keep the bait up. What the Foundation destroyed were the humanoid shells created by the fae. The whole point was to gain access to foundation personell so that they could pilot them, which is what they're doing right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just going to do a courtesy and clarify that while this is based on Bright's SCP-001 proposal, these aren't the fae in his canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck is going on

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting. I feel like there's one or two fairies puppeteering the whole operation. The Foundation didn't kill them, it just broke down the operation and made them retreat back to the fairy realm.

A couple questions: Why did the first D-class die? Why not just leave the building? Secondly, why aren't there way more host bodies (please give some name for these, it got confusing at points), given that even an ant is enough to trigger this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but
these things.

Mercenaries and hunters here built an adequate arena here…

Sounds clunky.

…pulls out an object resembling an AK-47 from behind it, seemingly from under its skirt.

I'm not entirely sure if it just resembles an AK-47 or if it actually is one, or where it was pulled from.

…the gun it was wielding,…

Not sure if tone. "the gun it had produced"? Wielding doesn't sound entirely neutral.

…the death was mostly caused by impact with the ground.

Just mostly?

05 EYES ONLY

O5, not naught. And it seems weird to me that these 13 that's canon, right? 13 O5s? folks would need a note among themselves on this one SCP that no researchers are allowed to read.


No vote and now I'm going to sleep. I prolly missed some stuff but I need to wake up tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, so all of the chats helped me with this, but mostly Wanderer's Library. I realize this is really out there so… hope you enjoy it.
